title: Immune and Nonimmune Hydrops Fetalis
authors: Hebert Taylor K, Simmons PM, Magann EF / Fanaroff & Martin 2025

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Hydrops Fetalis?
Hydrops fetalis (HF) คือภาวะที่ fetus มี fluid accumulation ≥2 compartments — เป็นหนึ่งใน most dramatic findings ใน perinatal medicine. สิ่งที่ต้องรู้: (1) IHF (10–24%) ลดลงมากหลัง RhoGAM — แต่ NIHF (76–90%) ยังมี perinatal mortality 50–90%; (2) MCA-PSV >1.5 MoM คือ threshold ส่ง fetal blood sampling — ต้องอ่าน Doppler เป็น; (3) NIHF differential ครอบคลุม >100 conditions — cardiovascular 21.7%, chromosomal 13.4%, hematologic 10.4%; (4) Exome sequencing เพิ่ม diagnostic yield NIHF ได้ 29% (Sparks NEJM 2020); (5) LOTUS study: IUT → neurodevelopmental impairment เพียง 4.8% ใน IHF — แต่ risk สูงมากใน severe HF; (6) Mirror syndrome (Ballantyne) — แม่ edema เหมือน fetus — indication for delivery.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
ให้นิยาม hydrops fetalis: diagnostic criteria, ≥2 compartments, classification IHF vs NIHF
อธิบาย Rh system genetics: chromosome locus, D/CcEe alleles, Fisher-Race nomenclature, DEL alleles
ระบุ pathophysiology IHF: alloimmunization → fetal hemolysis → severe anemia → HF; Hb threshold
วิเคราะห์ MCA-PSV: physiologic rationale, MoM thresholds (1.29/1.5/1.55), เริ่มตรวจ 16–18 wk
อธิบาย RhoGAM prophylaxis: dose, timing, indications ทุก trimester, Kleihauer-Betke
ระบุ IUT technique: blood type, HCT target, needle gauge, site, fetal loss rate, post-transfusion monitoring
ระบุ outcomes IHF: Netherlands/Scotland/Thailand data; LOTUS neurodevelopmental study
ระบุ Kell alloimmunization: mechanism ต่าง Rh (erythroid precursor destruction), management
อธิบาย NIHF pathophysiology: lymphatic vs oncotic vs venous mechanisms; etiologic categories
วางแผน antenatal evaluation NIHF: targeted US, echo, amniocentesis, exome sequencing (Sparks 2020)
ระบุ neonatal resuscitation ใน HF: anticipate difficult airway, drainage effusions, cause-specific Rx
1. Definition and Classification

Diagnostic Criteria — Hydrops Fetalis (Table 22.1 Fanaroff)
HF = excessive fetal fluid in ≥2 of the following spaces: (1) abdominal ascites (echolucent rim entire fetal abdomen transverse view); (2) pleural effusion (unilateral or bilateral lung border visualization); (3) pericardial effusion (nonphysiologic fluid ≥2 mm); (4) skin edema (subcutaneous edema ≥5 mm); (5) polyhydramnios (SDP ≥8 cm หรือ AFI ≥25 cm); (6) placentomegaly (placenta ≥6 cm). Diagnosis by ultrasound — ต้องประเมินทั้ง body cavities, placenta, amniotic fluid volume.
IHF (Immune HF): 10–24% of cases — due to RBC alloimmunization (mostly Rh-D, also Kell, Duffy, Kidd, MNSs)
NIHF (Nonimmune HF): 76–90% of cases — becoming increasingly predominant as IHF incidence ↓ with RhoGAM
Incidence: overall HF stable at 1/1500 – 1/3800 births since introduction of Rh immunoglobulin
Prognosis contrast: IHF with IUT → survival 86–94%; NIHF → perinatal mortality 50–90% depending on etiology
2. Immune Hydrops Fetalis — Background and Genetics
2.1 Historical Milestones
Diamond LK et al J Pediatr 1932: first recognition of erythroblastosis fetalis — neonatal hydrops + anemia + jaundice
Landsteiner & associates 1940: discovery of rhesus (Rh) antigen
Levine & coworkers JAMA 1941: Rh-negative woman exposed to Rh-positive RBCs → antibodies → transplacental passage → fetal hemolysis
Freda VJ et al 1964: anti-D immune globulin (RhoGAM) prevents sensitization in Rh-negative volunteers given Rh-positive blood
2.2 Rh System Genetics

Rh System — ระบบที่ซับซ้อนที่สุดของ Blood Groups
Locus: chromosome 1p36.1. Two genes: RHD (encodes D antigen) and RHCE (encodes C, c, E, e antigens). Fisher-Race nomenclature: DCE system — clinically preferred. D/CcEe: each parent contributes allele from D, C, E loci. D dominant over d; d = absence of D (no 'd antigen' exists). Cc/Ee alleles codominant — both expressed in heterozygote. DEL allele: strongly reduced/absent D — 30% Rh-negative Asians carry DEL (rare in Europeans); can still cause HDN. Partial D / weak D: qualitative/quantitative variants — clinically significant in pregnancy.
Rh-negative prevalence: European/North American 15–17%; African/Indian 3–8%; Asian 0.1–0.3% — ดังนั้นใน Thai population ความเสี่ยง IHF จาก Rh-D ต่ำกว่า Western countries มาก
Kell blood group: chromosome 7q33; ~20–25 antigens; K antigen most significant — ranks #3 after ABO + Rh for HDN risk
Kell mechanism: DIFFERENT from Rh — Kell IgG destroys erythroid precursors in fetal liver (suppresses erythropoiesis) vs Rh which attacks mature fetal RBCs
2.3 Sensitization Risk Factors

หมายเหตุ: 300 µg RhoGAM = suppress immune response to 30 mL fetal blood / 15 mL packed D-positive RBCs. หาก FMH มากกว่านี้ (large fetomaternal hemorrhage) → Kleihauer-Betke test เพื่อคำนวณ dose เพิ่มเติม.
3. Pathophysiology of IHF
3.1 Alloimmunization Cascade
FMH ≥0.01 mL detected in 3%, 12%, 46% of pregnancies in T1/T2/T3 respectively
Minimal volume for sensitization: 0.25 mL fetal blood
Risk of sensitization: 0.1 mL → 3%; 0.4 mL → 22%; delivery → 17%
ABO incompatibility (mother O; father A, B, or AB) reduces sensitization risk to 2% — fetal RBCs rapidly cleared from maternal circulation
30% Rh-negative individuals are non-responders — will not become sensitized even with exposure
3.2 Progression to Hydrops

IHF Progression — จาก Anemia ถึง Hydrops
Progressive RBC destruction: hemolysis → severe fetal anemia → extramedullary hematopoiesis (liver + spleen) → hepatomegaly → ↓ oncotic pressure (hypoalbuminemia). HF ensues when Hb deficit >7 g/dL (Nicolaides Semin Perinatol 1989). Better threshold: Hb <0.5 × median for GA (mean Hb: 10.6 g/dL at 18 wk → 13.8 g/dL at 40 wk). US progression: polyhydramnios + placentomegaly → hepatomegaly + pericardial effusion → ascites + scalp edema + pleural effusion → oligohydramnios. Mechanisms: high-output cardiac failure + liver dysfunction + fetal hypoxemia — likely combination of all three.
Survival impact: intravascular transfusion study: nonhydropic 94% vs hydropic 74% survival (Schumacher & Moise Obstet Gynecol 1996)
4. Detection and Monitoring — IHF
4.1 Antibody Screening
Initial prenatal visit: ABO type, Rh-D status, indirect Coombs test (antibody screen)
Anti-D titer ≥1:16 → requires further assessment; <1:16 → monthly titer surveillance
Paternal genotyping: heterozygous father → 50% fetal Rh-D negativity; homozygous → always Rh-D positive fetus
Cell-free fetal DNA from maternal blood: non-invasive fetal Rh-D typing — sensitivity/specificity 94–97% (Bombard Prenat Diagn 2011; Geifman-Holtzman Am J Obstet Gynecol 2006)
4.2 MCA-PSV Doppler

MCA-PSV — Standard of Care ในการ Predict Fetal Anemia
Physiology: fetal anemia → ↑ cardiac output (RV + LV output ↑ up to 45%, HR unchanged) → ↑ stroke volume → ↓ blood viscosity → ↑ MCA flow velocity (Poiseuille's law: velocity ∝ flow / cross-sectional area). Threshold: MCA-PSV >1.5 MoM → fetal blood sampling indicated (Mari et al N Engl J Med 2000;342:9). MoM thresholds (false-positive rate 12%): 1.29 MoM = mild anemia; 1.5 MoM = moderate; 1.55 MoM = severe. เริ่มตรวจ 16–18 wk; repeat every 1–2 wk. หลัง IUT: ใช้ higher cutoff 1.69 MoM สำหรับ timing second transfusion (Detti Am J Obstet Gynecol 2001). MCA-PSV แทนที่ amniocentesis OD450 แล้ว — non-invasive, accurate, preferred.
MCA: chosen because cerebral arteries respond rapidly to hypoxemia; easy to identify; low interobserver variability
Cordocentesis: most accurate but invasive — fetal loss up to 3%; reserved for when MCA-PSV >1.5 MoM to confirm anemia before IUT
OD450 amniocentesis: largely replaced by MCA Doppler — rarely used clinically anymore (Moise N Engl J Med 2006;355:192)
5. Intrauterine Transfusion (IUT)
5.1 Indications and Setup
MCA-PSV >1.5 MoM → cordocentesis to confirm anemia → if confirmed: IUT
Gestational age: typically 18–35 wk; first IUT by fetoscopy 1981 (Rodeck Lancet); now US-guided ultrasound
Blood product: Type O, Rh(D)-negative, CMV-negative, irradiated packed RBCs, crossmatched against maternal blood; HCT ~75% — high HCT = minimal volume transfused
Needle: 20–22 gauge echogenic tip; preferred site = umbilical vein near cord insertion into placenta; free loop cord / intrahepatic hepatic vein as alternatives
Fetal paralytic agent: vecuronium 0.1 mg/kg IM/IV — optional, prevents fetal movement
5.2 Targets and Monitoring
Post-transfusion HCT target: ~40%; if GA <24 wk: target 25%, repeat in 48h
Confirm fetal blood by mean corpuscular volume >110 µm³ (fetal blood) before transfusion
Hematocrit falls ~1 percentage point/day → follow-up transfusion needed in ~2 wk
Continuous fetal monitoring 4–6h post-procedure; longer if complications or uterine contractions
Antenatal corticosteroids: consider at viability (≥22 5/7 to 34 wk) — accelerate lung maturity in case urgent delivery needed
5.3 Intraperitoneal Transfusion (IPT)
Reserved for: GA <18 wk (vessels too small for intravascular) or intravascular technically impossible
Absorption: infused RBCs absorbed via subdiaphragmatic lymphatic lacunae + right lymphatic duct — requires fetal respiration; time to complete absorption ~150h
Bowman formula: volume = (weeks GA − 20) × 10 mL — CRITICAL: if intraperitoneal pressure > umbilical vein pressure → no blood flow → fetal death
Less effective when fetal hydrops present; survival improved from 24% to 42% over 22 years (hydropic group; Bennebroek Gravenhorst 1989)
5.4 Outcomes and Complications

IUT Outcomes — Netherlands / Scotland / Thailand / LOTUS
Netherlands retrospective (Van Kamp Acta Obstet Gynecol Scand 2004): 208 women, 593 transfusions; overall survival 86%; nonhydropic 92% vs hydropic 78%; <20 wk + hydropic 55%; Rh 89% vs Kell 58%; procedure loss 4.8%. Scotland (McGlone Arch Dis Child 2011): 116 pregnancies, 457 transfusions; birth-to-discharge survival 97.4%; procedure IUD rate 0.8% (1999–2004 era); 54% needed ≥1 top-up transfusion postnatally. Thailand (Tanvisut Ultrasound Obstet Gynecol 2020): 6650 cordocentesis; overall fetal loss 1.8%; procedure-attributable ~0.8%; risk factors: prolonged puncture site bleeding, fetal bradycardia, placental penetration. LOTUS study (Lindenburg Am J Obstet Gynecol 2012): 291 children at median 8.2 yr; CP 2.1%; severe DD 3.1%; bilateral deafness 1%; overall neurodevelopmental impairment 4.8%; only risk factor = severe fetal hydrops.
6. Suppressive Therapy — IVIG and Delivery Timing
6.1 IVIG for IHF <18–20 weeks
Indication: severe Rh alloimmunization <18–20 wk where IUT technically impossible (small vessels)
Mechanism: feedback inhibition maternal antibody synthesis + blockade reticuloendothelial Fc receptors + blockade placental antibody transport

Evidence limited and conflicting — some benefit if started before severe anemia (Gottvall Acta Obstet 1995); some show no effect on number transfusions; combination plasmapheresis + IVIG reported successful
Cost limiting factor — reserve for pregnancies <18–20 wk with severe anemia until IUT feasible at later GA
6.2 Delivery Timing
Mild alloimmunization: induce labor 37–38 wk; earlier if fetal lung maturity documented
Severe alloimmunization: last IUT at ~30–32 wk; delivery 32–34 wk with corticosteroids for lung maturity
Some experts: continue IUT through 35 wk; deliver at 37 wk with reasonable lung maturity
Delivery: tertiary facility with NICU + blood banking + laboratory; continuous EFM during labor; cord blood immediately for neonatal blood group + Rh type + direct Coombs + bili + Hb/HCT
7. Atypical RBC Antigens
7.1 Overview
As IHF from Rh-D ↓ with RhoGAM, relative importance of atypical antigens ↑ — no prophylactic immunoglobulin for these
Most significant atypical: Kell (K1, K2) > Duffy (Fy^a) > Kidd (Jk^a) > MNSs > Diego
Most IgM antibodies → don't cross placenta → no HDN; clinical concern = IgG-class antibodies
7.2 Kell Alloimmunization

Kell — Mechanism ต่าง Rh: Suppresses Erythropoiesis ไม่ใช่แค่ Hemolysis
Kell IgG antibodies destroy erythroid precursors in fetal liver (macrophage-mediated) → ↓ early RBC proliferation → severe fetal anemia with less bilirubin release than Rh (fewer mature RBCs hemolyzed) → OD450 unreliable — do NOT use. 91% population Kell-negative; only 5% form Kell antibodies after transfusion → disorder relatively rare. Management: paternal genotyping first — if father Kell-negative → fetus unaffected, no further workup. Monitoring with MCA-PSV >1.5 MoM = threshold for anemia (same as Rh; Van Dongen Ultrasound OG 2005). Kell alloimmunized pregnancies: survival 58% vs Rh 89% (Netherlands study) — worse prognosis.
8. Nonimmune Hydrops Fetalis (NIHF)
8.1 Pathophysiology

NIHF Pathophysiology — สามเส้นทางสู่ Fluid Accumulation
HF occurs when interstitial fluid production > lymphatic return → fluid accumulates in interstitial spaces. Three major mechanisms: (1) Reduced lymphatic flow: pulmonary masses, lymphatic malformations, thoracic compression; (2) Lower plasma oncotic pressure: GI anomalies, IEM, liver failure → hypoproteinemia; (3) Increased central venous pressure: chromosomal anomalies, heart failure (volume overload or anemia), compensatory hypoxia. NIHF rarely resolves spontaneously; worse prognosis earlier in pregnancy.
8.2 Etiology — Frequency Distribution

rows: Structural CHD (hypoplasias, AV canal, TGA, VSD/ASD, Ebstein); arrhythmias (SVT, flutter, heart block, long QT); cardiomyopathy
No cause identified — prognosis very poor (mortality approaches 100%)
CDH, CPAM, pulmonary sequestration, chylothorax, airway obstruction; skeletal dysplasias (thanatophoric)
Alpha-thalassemia (Asia!), FMH, G6PD deficiency, RBC aplasia, pyruvate kinase deficiency
Noonan, arthrogryposis, multiple pterygium syndrome, Pena-Shokeir, myotonic dystrophy
Parvovirus B19 (aplastic crisis), CMV, syphilis, rubella, toxoplasma, coxsackievirus
TTTS (donor twin), acardiac twin, umbilical vein thrombosis, cord knot, chorionic vein thrombosis
Urethral atresia/stenosis, posterior urethral valve, Finnish nephrosis, prune belly
Gaucher, GM1 gangliosidosis, sialidosis, MPS IVA, mucolipidosis
Duodenal atresia, volvulus, meconium peritonitis, biliary atresia, hepatic vascular malformations

blocks.text:
Alpha-Thalassemia — NIHF เหตุสำคัญในเอเชีย
Hb Barts hydrops (4-gene deletion α-thalassemia): most common cause NIHF ในเอเชียตะวันออกเฉียงใต้ รวมถึงไทย (hematologic 10.4% NIHF category). Hb Barts (γ₄) = high O₂ affinity → severe tissue hypoxia → HF. ต่างจาก IHF: Hb Barts → no functional Hb → in utero death ส่วนใหญ่ถ้าไม่ได้ IUT + BMT. Carrier screening สำคัญมากใน Thai context — maternal CBC + Hb typing + parental carrier testing ก่อนคลอด.
9. Antenatal Evaluation of NIHF
9.1 Workup Components (Table 22.5)

rows: Age, parity, GA; hereditary/metabolic disease; anemia (carrier α-thal in Thai families); recent infections; medication use
CBC; blood type + Rh + indirect Coombs; viral titers (parvovirus B19, toxo, syphilis, rubella, CMV, HSV); aneuploidy screening; cell-free fetal DNA; metabolic/autoimmune if indicated
Cardiac malformations; arrhythmias (SVT → adenosine consideration; heart block → maternal anti-Ro/La)
Anatomic abnormalities (cystic hygroma common <20 wk → chromosomal); extent edema/effusions; cardiac displacement; lung hypoplasia; MCA-PSV (rule out anemia)
Karyotype; viral culture or PCR; lung maturity if near delivery
Karyotype; CBC + type; Hb electrophoresis; viral culture/PCR; metabolic testing
Viral culture/PCR; protein count; cell count + cytology (lymphocytes → chylothorax)
Unexplained NIHF — 29% yield (Sparks NEJM 2020); RASopathy most common (30% of genetic diagnoses)

blocks.text: 9.2 Key Finding: Cystic Hygroma
Cystic hygroma common before 20 wk — often associated with chromosomal anomalies
Turner syndrome (45 XO): most common chromosomal cause in early-onset NIHF; also trisomies 21, 18, 13 (common before 20 wk)
Intrathoracic masses → restrictive fetal swallowing → polyhydramnios → uterine size > GA → prompts ultrasound
9.3 Exome Sequencing in NIHF

Sparks TN et al N Engl J Med 2020;383:1746 — Game-Changer สำหรับ NIHF Diagnosis
Study: 127 fetuses with unexplained NIHF underwent exome sequencing (2018–2020). Result: 37/127 (29%) had identifiable genetic disorder. RASopathy (Ras/MAPK pathway — Noonan syndrome family) most common: 30% of identified diagnoses; followed by IEM + musculoskeletal 11%; lymphatic 8%; neurodevelopmental 8%; cardiovascular 8%; hematologic 8%. Chromosomal microarray: current studies suggest LOW utility in NIHF (Mardy Prenat Diagn 2020) — exome sequencing preferred. Clinical implication: exome sequencing ใน unexplained NIHF → increases specific diagnosis → guides counseling + recurrence risk.
10. Management of NIHF
10.1 Antepartum Management
Options based on GA + identified cause: termination of pregnancy; invasive fetal intervention; conservative management
Fetal surveillance: weekly or twice-weekly NST + BPP; serial growth ultrasound; early subspecialty consultation (neonatology, pediatric surgery, cardiology)

Mirror Syndrome (Ballantyne Syndrome) — Indication for Delivery
Definition: mother develops similar findings to fetus — generalized maternal edema, often pulmonary involvement — 'mirroring' hydropic fetus and placenta. Pathogenesis unknown: hydropic placenta → systemic inflammatory response from ↑ trophoblastic debris in maternal blood → maternal edema. Clinical recognition critical: maternal pulmonary edema + generalized edema in the context of fetal hydrops → delivery indicated regardless of GA. Can mimic preeclampsia — distinguish by context of hydrops.
10.2 Invasive Fetal Interventions
Shunt placement: pleuropleural/vesicoamniotic shunt for diaphragmatic hernia, CPAM, pulmonary sequestration, posterior urethral valve → notable survival improvement in select conditions
Paracentesis / thoracentesis: diagnostic only — fluid reaccumulates rapidly; shunt placement preferred for persistent effusions
In utero surgery: CDH, CPAM, extralobar sequestration — benefit in select cases
IUT: for hematologic causes (alpha-thalassemia, parvovirus B19 with aplastic crisis) — significant survival benefit in these specific etiologies
10.3 Delivery Indications

rows: GA >34 wk + mature fetal lung profile
Hospitalize: fetal skin thickening, pericardial effusion, nonreactive NST, BPP <6, decreased fetal movement, anticipated imminent delivery
CS for routine obstetrical indications only (not HF per se)

blocks.text: 10.4 Prognosis
Structural defect identified → perinatal mortality approaches 100% — most severe etiologies
Idiopathic NIHF → similarly very poor — no treatable cause
Better prognosis: later diagnosis + treatable cause (parvovirus with IUT, SVT with anti-arrhythmics, isolated chylothorax with shunt)
NIHF identified early <24 wk → worse prognosis than later
Recurrence: rare (especially non-hereditary causes); hereditary causes → recurrence risk per specific Mendelian pattern
Definitive diagnosis in only ~67% of NIHF cases — 33% remain unexplained even after full workup
11. NICU Practical — รับทารก Hydrops
ก่อนคลอด: รับ history จาก OB: IHF vs NIHF? เหตุที่ทราบ? GA? UA Doppler / IUT done? Antenatal steroids given? สั่ง blood product ล่วงหน้า
Anticipate difficult resuscitation: massive pleural effusion + ascites + skin edema → severe respiratory compromise ตั้งแต่คลอด → intubation ทันที + chest drain พร้อม; team ≥3 คน
Airway: ท้องอืดจาก ascites → diaphragm high → FRC ลด → intubate early; paracentesis ทันทีถ้า abdominal compartment syndrome
Drainage of effusions: bilateral pleural effusions → bilateral thoracentesis หรือ chest tube ถ้า large effusion → ช่วย lung expansion อย่างมีนัยสำคัญ

IHF neonatal management: CBC + reticulocyte + bilirubin + direct Coombs จาก cord blood; ถ้า severe anemia → immediate exchange transfusion หรือ top-up transfusion; phototherapy aggressive; monitor bili q2-4h แรก
Electrolytes + albumin: hypoproteinemia → 3rd space loss; correct albumin + maintain oncotic pressure; edema → Na/fluid management ระมัดระวัง
NIHF cause-specific Rx: SVT → adenosine/digoxin; heart block → isoprenaline; parvovirus → IVIG + IUT ถ้า pre-delivery; alpha-thal → immediate hematology consult
Respiratory support: HFV ถ้า conventional ไม่เพียงพอ; iNO ถ้า PPHN component; lung hypoplasia common in thoracic-mass HF
Chromosomal workup: karyotype/CMA/exome sequencing ถ้ายังไม่ได้ prenatal — ช่วย counseling family
Family counseling: prognosis ขึ้นกับ etiology อย่างมาก — IHF ที่ได้ IUT: ดีมาก; NIHF ไม่ทราบสาเหตุ: grave prognosis; แจ้ง Palliative care team ถ้าเหมาะสม
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — MCA-PSV >1.5 MoM: Threshold ส่ง Fetal Blood Sampling ทันที
MCA-PSV แทนที่ OD450 amniocentesis แล้ว ใน Rh alloimmunization. Threshold >1.5 MoM → cordocentesis → ถ้ายืนยัน anemia → IUT. เริ่มตรวจ 16–18 wk; ทำซ้ำ 1–2 wk interval. Post-IUT: ใช้ cutoff 1.69 MoM สำหรับ timing 2nd transfusion (Detti 2001). ระวัง: false-positive rate 12% — ไม่ transfuse โดยไม่ยืนยัน fetal anemia ก่อน.

PEARL 2 — Kell ≠ Rh: OD450 ห้ามใช้ใน Kell Alloimmunization
Kell IgG ทำลาย erythroid precursors (ไม่ใช่ mature RBCs) → น้อย bilirubin release → OD450 เป็น false reassurance และห้ามใช้. ใช้ MCA-PSV >1.5 เหมือน Rh แทน. ตรวจ paternal Kell genotype ก่อน — ถ้าพ่อ Kell-negative → fetus unaffected, ไม่ต้อง workup เพิ่ม. Survival worse ใน Kell (58%) vs Rh (89%) — ต้องเฝ้าระวังสูง.

PEARL 3 — NIHF ใน Thai Context: Alpha-Thalassemia Top Differential
Cardiovascular 21.7% คือ etiology อันดับ 1 ระดับ global แต่ใน Thailand + Southeast Asia ต้อง top-of-mind ถึง alpha-thalassemia (4-gene deletion = Hb Barts) ซึ่งอยู่ใน hematologic 10.4%. Hb Barts มี extreme O₂ affinity → tissue anoxia → HF + in utero death. Carrier screening พ่อแม่ก่อนคลอดด้วย CBC + Hb typing ช่วย prevent. ถ้า NIHF ไม่ทราบสาเหตุใน Thai infant → Hb electrophoresis ทันที.

PEARL 4 — Mirror Syndrome ใน NIHF: แม่ก็ Edema เหมือนลูก → Deliver ทันที
Mirror syndrome (Ballantyne): mother develops systemic edema + pulmonary edema mirroring fetal hydrops — คล้าย severe preeclampsia. Mechanism: hydropic placenta → ↑ trophoblastic debris → systemic inflammatory response. Recognition: maternal edema worsening parallel to fetal hydrops → indication for delivery regardless of GA. อย่าหลงคิดว่าเป็นแค่ preeclampsia — ตรวจ fetal status + hydrops ด้วยเสมอ.

PEARL 5 — NIHF Delivery Room: Anticipate Bilateral Pleural + Ascites → Have Chest Drain Ready
Hydropic neonate = most difficult DR resuscitation. Massive pleural effusions → lung collapse → intubation ไม่ช่วยถ้าไม่ drain. เตรียม bilateral thoracentesis needles + chest drain sets + ทีม 3+ คน. Ascites ขนาดใหญ่ → ยก diaphragm → paracentesis ก่อน ventilate. ถ้า IHF: cord blood direct Coombs + bili + Hb ทันที — exchange transfusion ถ้า severe anemia. ไม่มีเวลา hesitate ใน delivery room.
13. Evidence Update — Key Studies
Diamond LK et al J Pediatr 1932;1:269: first description erythroblastosis fetalis — established IHF as clinical entity
Mari G et al N Engl J Med 2000;342(1):9 (Collaborative Group): MCA-PSV for noninvasive diagnosis fetal anemia; optimal threshold 1.5 MoM moderate/severe; false-positive 12%; replaced OD450
Moise KJ N Engl J Med 2006;355:192: editorial 'time to put away the needles' — endorsing MCA-PSV over amniocentesis OD450 for fetal anemia detection
Van Kamp IL et al Acta Obstet Gynecol Scand 2004;83:731 (Netherlands): 208 women, 593 IUTs; overall survival 86%; hydropic 78%; Kell 58% vs Rh 89%; procedure loss 4.8%
McGlone L et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2011;96:F69 (Scotland): 116 pregnancies, 457 transfusions; birth–discharge survival 97.4%; procedure IUD 0.8% (1999–2004)

Tanvisut R et al Ultrasound Obstet Gynecol 2020;56(5):664 (Thailand, Maharaj Nakorn CM): 6650 cordocentesis; overall fetal loss 1.8%; procedure-attributable ~0.8%; risk factors: prolonged bleeding, bradycardia, placental penetration

Lindenburg IT et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 2012;206(2):141.e1 (LOTUS): 291 children at median 8.2 yr post-IUT; neurodevelopmental impairment 4.8%; CP 2.1%; severe DD 3.1%; deafness 1%; only risk = severe HF
Sparks TN et al N Engl J Med 2020;383(18):1746: 127 unexplained NIHF, exome sequencing → 29% yield; RASopathy 30% of genetic diagnoses; low utility chromosomal microarray
Geifman-Holtzman O et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 2006;195(4):1163: meta-analysis noninvasive fetal Rh genotyping from maternal blood — sensitivity/specificity 94–97%; reduces need for invasive testing
Bombard AT et al Prenat Diagn 2011;13:802: cell-free fetal DNA for non-sensitized Rh-negative women — fetal Rh-D genotyping from maternal plasma
Van Dongen H et al Ultrasound Obstet Gynecol 2005;25:341: MCA-PSV >1.5 MoM predictive severe fetal anemia in Kell alloimmunization — same threshold as Rh; OD450 unreliable Kell
Detti L et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 2001;185:1048: Doppler timing for 2nd IUT: post-transfusion MCA-PSV use higher cutoff 1.69 MoM
Schumacher B & Moise KJ Obstet Gynecol 1996;88:137: survival intravascular transfusions: nonhydropic 94% vs hydropic 74% — HF significantly worsens prognosis
Mardy AH et al Prenat Diagn 2020;40(4):492: chromosomal microarray utility NIHF: low diagnostic yield; exome sequencing superior for NIHF genetic diagnosis
Bellini C & Hennekam RCM Am J Med Genet 2012;158A:597: NIHF short review etiology + pathology; cardiovascular 21.7% largest category; idiopathic 17.8%
Paternoster DM et al Fetal Diagn Ther 2006;21(1):92: Ballantyne (mirror) syndrome case report; pathogenesis: hydropic placenta → systemic inflammation from trophoblastic debris
14. Self-Check Q&A
Q: Hydrops fetalis คืออะไร และต้องพบ fluid ใน compartments ใดอย่างน้อยกี่ compartment?

ตอบ: HF = excessive fetal fluid ใน ≥2 of: ascites, pleural effusion, pericardial effusion, skin edema, polyhydramnios, placentomegaly. วินิจฉัยด้วย US. แบ่งเป็น IHF (10–24%) จาก RBC alloimmunization และ NIHF (76–90%) จากสาเหตุอื่น.
Q: MCA-PSV Doppler ทำงานอย่างไรในการ predict fetal anemia?

ตอบ: Fetal anemia → ↑ cardiac output + ↓ blood viscosity → ↑ MCA flow velocity. Threshold >1.5 MoM = moderate-severe anemia → cordocentesis + IUT (Mari NEJM 2000). Thresholds: 1.29 MoM mild, 1.5 moderate, 1.55 severe. เริ่ม 16–18 wk; repeat 1–2 wk. Post-IUT: cutoff 1.69 MoM.
Q: ทำไม OD450 amniocentesis ห้ามใช้ใน Kell alloimmunization?

ตอบ: Kell IgG ทำลาย erythroid precursors (ไม่ใช่ mature RBCs) → น้อย bilirubin production → OD450 ต่ำทั้งที่มี severe fetal anemia = false reassurance. ใช้ MCA-PSV >1.5 MoM แทน (Van Dongen 2005). ตรวจ paternal Kell genotype ก่อน — ถ้าพ่อ Kell-negative → ไม่ต้อง monitor.
Q: RhoGAM dose: จะให้กี่ไมโครกรัมและเมื่อไหร่?

ตอบ: 300 µg: standard dose ≥12 wk GA; ≥12 wk ectopic/abortion; all 2nd/3rd trimester indications; antepartum at 28 wk; within 72h delivery ถ้า neonate Rh-D positive. 50–120 µg: first trimester abortion/ectopic/CVS ≤12 wk. 300 µg = suppress immune response to 30 mL fetal blood / 15 mL packed pRBCs.
Q: ระบุ top 3 etiologies ของ NIHF พร้อม % และ key example แต่ละ category

ตอบ: 1. Cardiovascular 21.7%: SVT, AV canal, hypoplastic heart, cardiomyopathy. 2. Idiopathic 17.8%: ไม่พบสาเหตุ — prognosis สุดเลวร้าย. 3. Chromosomal 13.4%: Turner 45,X (cystic hygroma <20 wk), trisomy 21/18/13. ใน Thai context ต้องเพิ่ม alpha-thalassemia (hematologic 10.4%) เป็น top differential เสมอ.
Q: Exome sequencing มี diagnostic yield เท่าไรใน NIHF ที่ยังไม่ทราบสาเหตุ?

ตอบ: Sparks NEJM 2020: 127 unexplained NIHF → exome sequencing → 29% ได้ genetic diagnosis. RASopathy (Noonan syndrome family) พบมากสุด 30% ของ genetic diagnoses. Chromosomal microarray: low utility. สรุป: เพิ่ม exome sequencing ใน workup NIHF ที่ไม่ทราบสาเหตุ.
Q: Mirror syndrome คืออะไร และจัดการอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Mirror (Ballantyne) syndrome: แม่เกิด generalized edema + pulmonary involvement เหมือน fetal hydrops — hydropic placenta → systemic inflammation. Indication for delivery regardless of GA. อย่าสับสนกับ preeclampsia — ตรวจ fetal hydrops ด้วยเสมอถ้า maternal edema รุนแรงในช่วง pregnancy.
Q: IUT blood product specification: ต้องใช้ blood ชนิดใด?

ตอบ: Type O, Rh(D)-negative, CMV-negative, irradiated packed RBCs, crossmatched against maternal blood. HCT ~75% (high HCT = minimal volume transfused). Confirm fetal blood sample ด้วย MCV >110 µm³. Post-transfusion HCT target ~40% (GA <24 wk: 25%, repeat 48h). HCT ↓ ~1%/day → repeat IUT ~2 wk.
Q: NIHF ที่มี bilateral pleural effusions ใน DR: management approach?

ตอบ: Anticipate difficult resuscitation: intubate ทันที (lung collapse จาก massive effusion). Bilateral thoracentesis ทันทีถ้า large effusions → significant lung re-expansion. Paracentesis ถ้า ascites กด diaphragm. Team ≥3 คน. ถ้า IHF: cord blood CBC + Coombs + bili → exchange transfusion ถ้า severe anemia. ถ้า NIHF: workup cause immediately.
Q: LOTUS study: outcomes ของ children ที่ได้รับ IUT สำหรับ IHF?

ตอบ: Lindenburg Am J Obstet Gynecol 2012: 291 children, median age 8.2 yr: CP 2.1%; severe DD 3.1%; bilateral deafness 1%; overall neurodevelopmental impairment 4.8%. Only risk factor = severe fetal HF. สรุป: IUT ใน IHF → relative safety; แต่ severe HF prenatal = ↑ neurodevelopmental risk — counseling family สำคัญ.

Day 120 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.23
บทต่อไป: Chapter 23 — Amniotic Fluid Volume. เตรียมตัว: regulation amniotic fluid (fetal urine production, fetal swallowing, intramembranous/transmembranous flow); polyhydramnios (definition AFI >25 cm; causes: GI atresia, CNS, maternal DM, idiopathic); oligohydramnios (SDP <2 cm; causes: PPROM, renal agenesis, growth restriction, postterm); ultrasound measurements (AFI vs SDP evidence); amnioinfusion; amniocentesis indications; impact on neonatal outcomes (pulmonary hypoplasia in oligohydramnios).
